inputs.label: Workpiece Material
inputs.businessContext: Material type determines recommended cutting speed range. Based on ISO 513 classification and machining handbooks (e.g., Machining Data Handbook, 3rd ed.).

inputs.label: Tool Material
inputs.businessContext: Tool material affects cutting speed capability. Higher hardness materials allow higher speeds. Reference: ISO 513.

inputs.label: Operation Type
inputs.businessContext: Different operations have different speed recommendations. Based on SME Tool Engineering Handbook.

inputs.label: Cutting Speed
inputs.businessContext: Actual cutting speed used. Must be within recommended range for material/tool combination. Reference: ISO 3685.

inputs.label: Tool Diameter
inputs.businessContext: Diameter of cutting tool (for milling/drilling). Affects spindle speed calculation. Reference: ISO 3002.

inputs.label: Spindle Speed
inputs.businessContext: Rotational speed of spindle. Calculated from cutting speed and diameter. Reference: ISO 230.

inputs.label: Feed Rate
inputs.businessContext: Feed per revolution. Affects surface finish and tool life. Reference: ISO 3685.

inputs.label: Depth of Cut
inputs.businessContext: Radial or axial depth. Influences cutting forces and power. Reference: ISO 3002.

inputs.label: Tool Life
inputs.businessContext: Expected tool life before replacement. Used for cost analysis. Reference: Taylor's tool life equation.

inputs.label: Machine Power
inputs.businessContext: Available spindle power. Must exceed required cutting power. Reference: ISO 3002.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data. Adjusts output reliability. Common in risk analysis.

rules: cuttingSpeed must be between recommended min and max for given workpieceMaterial and toolMaterial
feedRate must be within recommended range for operationType

thresholds.cuttingSpeedHigh: cuttingSpeed > 1.2 * recommendedMaxSpeed -> 'Cutting speed exceeds recommended maximum by more than 20%. Risk of tool failure.'
thresholds.cuttingSpeedLow: cuttingSpeed < 0.8 * recommendedMinSpeed -> 'Cutting speed below recommended minimum. Inefficient material removal.'
thresholds.toolLifeLow: toolLife < 10 -> 'Tool life very short. Consider reducing cutting speed or feed.'
thresholds.powerExceeded: requiredCuttingPower > machinePower -> 'Required cutting power exceeds machine capacity. Reduce depth of cut or feed.'

suggestedActions: If cutting speed too high: reduce speed or use more wear-resistant tool material.
If cutting speed too low: increase speed to improve productivity.
If tool life too short: reduce cutting speed or feed, or apply coolant.
If power exceeded: reduce depth of cut or feed rate, or use a machine with higher power.
